full_text:
WeCoded 2026: Frontend Art 💜

 
 

 

This is a submission for the 2026 WeCoded Challenge: Frontend Art

 
 
 👨‍🦰👩‍🦳 Frontend Art Submission — Gender Beyond Boundaries

I built an interactive 3D experience using Next.js and Three.js that visually represents gender equality across professions.

The project features two avatars — a male on the left and a female on the right — dynamically showcasing 10 different professions. Alongside the visuals, contextual metadata appears, including real-world role models for each gender, reinforcing that capability and excellence are not defined by gender.

👉 Live Preview

👉 Github

 
 
 💡 Inspiration

This project is a tribute to gender equality — a statement that professions, skills, and ambitions belong to everyone.

Growing up, many of us are subtly (or explicitly) told what we can or cannot become based on gender. I wanted to challenge that narrative using immersive web technology.

Each profession highlights real individuals who broke barriers, showing that talent, courage, and intelligence are universal traits. The dual-avatar system visually reinforces a simple but powerful idea:

There is no “male job” or “female job” — only human potential.

 
 
 🧠 Featured Professions & Role Models

 
 
 📰 Journalist

A fearless seeker of truth shaping public narratives.

Male: James Baldwin

Female: Nellie Bly

 
 
 🌾 Farmer

The backbone of global food security and sustainability.

Male: George Washington Carver

Female: Karen Washington

 
 
 🚒 Firefighter

A symbol of courage and selfless service.

Male: Patrick Hardison

Female: Molly Williams

 
 
 💻 Software Engineer

Architects of the digital world.

Male: Alan Turing

Female: Ada Lovelace

 
 
 🔧 Mechanic

Masters of machines and precision engineering.

Male: Henry Ford

Female: Patrice Banks

 
 
 🏥 Nurse

The heartbeat of healthcare systems.

Male: James Derham

Female: Florence Nightingale

 
 
 ✈️ Pilot

Leaders navigating the skies.

Male: Charles Lindbergh

Female: Amelia Earhart

 
 
 👮 Police Officer

Guardians of justice and public safety.

Male: August Vollmer

Female: Alice Stebbins Wells

 
 
 🛍️ Retail Salesperson

Drivers of commerce and community connection.

Male: Marshall Field

Female: Madam C.J. Walker

 
 
 🩺 Doctor

Leaders in healing and medical innovation.

Male: Jonas Salk

Female: Elizabeth Blackwell

 
 
 💻 My Code

Built with:

Next.js for structure and performance

Three.js for immersive 3D rendering

Custom logic for dynamic avatar switching and metadata display

👉 Source Code

 
 
 ✨ Final Thought

This project isn’t just about visuals — it’s about perspective.

By placing male and female avatars side by side in every profession, I wanted users to feel equality, not just read about it. Technology has the power to shape narratives, and this is my small contribution toward a more inclusive one.

 
 
 🚀 Performance & Optimization Note

As of this submission, I am actively working on further optimizing the asset delivery. Since high-quality Avatar GLB files can be heavy, they currently take a moment to fully render depending on the connection.

Thanks for reading! 🙌

Until next time, 🫡

Usman Awan (your friendly dev 🚀)
